manufacturer: Hirose Electric (also sourced from I-PEX, Murata, and others under U.FL / UMCC / AMC designations)
package: SMD RF Coaxial Connector, 2-terminal (center pin + shell ground)

pins.name: RF
pins.function: Center conductor – RF signal, 50 Ω characteristic impedance

pins.name: GND
pins.function: Outer shell / ground – four symmetrical SMD tabs or continuous ring pad, RF return path and mechanical anchor

example_application.description: Used as a board-mounted RF coaxial receptacle for antenna connections on compact PCBs (e.g. GPS, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, cellular modules). The mating plug (MHF-1 plug / U.FL plug) snaps into the receptacle providing a 50 Ω transmission path from DC to 6 GHz.
example_application.typical_schematic_notes: Place as close as possible to the RF device. Route the 50 Ω microstrip or coplanar-waveguide trace directly from the center pin (pad 1) to the RF IC without vias. Tie the four shell pads (pad 2) directly to the ground plane with multiple vias immediately adjacent to the pads. Keep the courtyard clear of other components. Recommended PCB stackup: 0.8 mm or 1.0 mm FR-4 with a controlled-impedance top layer. Maximum insertion/mating cycles: 30 per Hirose specification.

raw_text:
MHF-1 / U.FL Miniature RF Coaxial Connector

Manufacturer: Hirose Electric Co., Ltd. (equivalent: I-PEX 20279, Murata MM8430, IPEX MHF-1)

Description:
The MHF-1 (also marketed as U.FL, UMCC, and AMC) is a sub-miniature snap-on RF coaxial connector designed for surface-mount assembly on printed circuit boards. It provides a reliable 50 Ω coaxial interface for high-frequency signals up to 6 GHz and is widely used in compact wireless modules, laptops, IoT devices, GPS receivers, and cellular equipment.

Pin Description:
  Pin 1 – RF (Center Conductor): The signal pin, connected to the inner conductor of the mating coaxial cable or board-to-board connection. Carries the RF signal with 50 Ω impedance.
  Pin 2 – GND (Outer Shell / Shield): Four symmetrical SMD solder tabs extending from the outer metal shell. These tabs provide both the RF ground/return path and mechanical retention on the PCB. Electrically they are all connected to the outer shell.

Electrical Specifications:
  Characteristic Impedance:  50 Ω
  Frequency Range:           DC to 6 GHz
  VSWR:                      ≤ 1.3 (DC – 3 GHz), ≤ 1.5 (3 – 6 GHz)
  Insertion Loss:            ≤ 0.3 dB (DC – 3 GHz)
  RF Isolation:              ≥ 20 dB
  Working Voltage:           50 V AC/DC (max)
  Withstanding Voltage:      100 V AC (1 minute)
  Maximum Current:           0.5 A
  Contact Resistance:        Center conductor ≤ 30 mΩ, Outer conductor ≤ 5 mΩ
  Insulation Resistance:     ≥ 500 MΩ

Mechanical Specifications:
  Connector Style:           Snap-on, push-on coaxial receptacle
  Mating Height (assembled): 2.5 mm max above PCB surface
  Body Footprint:            approx 2.0 mm × 2.5 mm (Hirose U.FL-R-SMT)
  Center Pin Pad Diameter:   ~0.6 mm
  Outer Shell Pad Arrangement: 4 symmetrical pads, outer envelope ~2.0 mm × 2.5 mm
  Mating Cycles:             30 cycles minimum
  Operating Temperature:     -40 °C to +85 °C
  Storage Temperature:       -40 °C to +85 °C
  Material – Contacts:       Copper alloy, gold-plated
  Material – Shell:          Copper alloy, nickel-plated
  Material – Insulator:      PTFE or LCP
  Weight:                    approx 0.03 g

Footprint / Land Pattern (per Hirose recommendation):
  Center pad:  Circular, diameter 0.6 mm (for center signal conductor)
  Shell pads:  4 × rectangular pads placed symmetrically at N/S/E/W positions, each approx 0.8 mm × 0.5 mm, on a ~1.9 mm pitch from center
  Courtyard:   3.0 mm × 3.0 mm
  Keep-out:    Maintain 0.5 mm clearance from courtyard edge

Packaging:
  Tape & Reel: 1000 pcs/reel (Hirose standard)
  Tray: available on request
  RoHS compliant

Related / Compatible Part Numbers:
  Hirose:  U.FL-R-SMT(10), U.FL-R-SMT-1(10)
  I-PEX:   20279-001E, MHF-1 series
  Murata:  MM8430 series
  IPEX:    MHF-1
  Generic designation: U.FL receptacle, UMCC, AMC connector
